steps.title: Average requires only sum and count
steps.content:
The average (or mean) is the total sum divided by the number of items. We have been given exactly what we need:

$$\text{Average} = \frac{\text{Sum}}{\text{Count}} = \frac{90}{10} = 9\text{ boxes per student}$$

This calculation uses only the total (90) and count (10), both provided in the problem.

steps.title: Other measures require individual data we do not have
steps.content:
The median, maximum, and minimum each require knowing the individual sales for each student—information we lack. Consider:

  • Median: To find the median of 10 values, we must arrange them in order and locate the middle value. We cannot determine this from the sum alone.
  • Maximum: Without knowing each student's total, the maximum could range from 9 boxes (if evenly distributed) to 90 boxes (if one student sold all). It is indeterminate.
  • Minimum: Likewise indeterminate without individual values—could be 0 or any number up to 9.
Only the average can be calculated from the sum and count. That matches choice A — A is correct.
